hypothesis_tested: grep-based dedup check and verify-before-FAIL for structural check
notes: v2 improved dedup (no re-processing of already-linted files observed). However a new failure mode emerged in the April 13 batch: 6 files (lodash, microsoft-typescript, openai-whisper, prettier, psf-requests-combined, psf-requests-manual) scored PASS despite having ❌ on checks 2-4 (required fields, description quality, fix diversity). The Gemma check was silently skipped ('Skipped in v2 (structural focus)') and the aggregation logic defaulted to PASS when only Check 1 passed. Root cause: the scoring aggregation rule 'PASS = All 4 structural checks PASS + Gemma ≥80%' is being misread — the model interprets 'structural checks' as only Check 1 and skips the content checks. Additionally, when Gemma is unavailable (gemma4:e4b not found, April 14 10:32 error), Check 5 returns 0% but this doesn't cascade correctly. v3 will replace the aggregation block with an explicit numbered decision tree and add a Gemma-unavailable fallback rule.
